dashboard.label: About
dashboard.definition: {"title":"About","description":"Setup, documentation, and source links","visualizations":{"about_md":{"type":"splunk.markdown","options":{"markdown":"# Oura Health for Splunk An end-to-end pipeline and Dashboard Studio app for visualizing Oura Ring data — sleep, heart rate, activity, wellness, and ring/battery status. > **This is the legacy single-vendor app.** New work lives in the multi-vendor **wearables** platform (wearables app + TA-oura / TA-garmin add-ons), which supersedes this app. The Oura **fetcher** now lives in **TA-oura** (`tools/oura_to_hec_with_phi.py`). ## Quick start 1. **Create an Oura developer app** and mint an OAuth token at developer.ouraring.com. 2. **Create the `oura` index** — Settings → Indexes → New Index (leave the index type as **Events**, the default). 3. **Enable HTTP Event Collector (HEC)** — Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → Global Settings (enable it; note the port). 4. **Create a HEC token tied to the `oura` index** — Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → New Token; set its allowed/default index to `oura`. 5. **Run the ingest script** (`oura_to_hec_with_phi.py`) to pull the Oura API into HEC (OAuth2 + PKCE, incremental, deduped); schedule it via cron. 6. **Install the two companion viz apps** (below) so the hypnogram and charge-status ring render. The full step-by-step guide — Oura API, HEC URL variations, cron, backfill, troubleshooting — is in **INSTALL.md** in the source repo. ## Source & documentation - **App source, ingest script & INSTALL.md:** github.com/narwhaldc/oura-health-splunk - **Full setup guide:** INSTALL.md ## Companion custom visualizations (install alongside) Install these GA versions so the hypnogram and charge-status ring render: - **hypnogram_viz 1.0.1** — sleep & HR-zone lane timelines: github.com/narwhaldc/hypnogram_viz - **charge_ring_viz 1.0.0** — the ring charge-status gauge: github.com/narwhaldc/charge_ring_viz ## GA release set - **oura_health 2.0.1** (this app) · **hypnogram_viz 1.0.1** · **charge_ring_viz 1.0.0** - All three pass Splunk Cloud AppInspect. Install the two viz add-ons before (or alongside) this app; a full Splunk restart makes the custom viz JS render. ## Scope & known limitations - **Single-user by design** — it assumes one Oura account owner who is also the only person with access to the `oura` index (and therefore its personal health information, PHI). Multi-user access isolation and role-based access control (RBAC) are planned future work. - **Daily step target is fixed at 10,000** — Oura's API provides no step goal, so the Activity step gauge uses a placeholder 10K; it will become per-user once the user-lookup / RBAC work lands. (Distance and active-calorie goals come from Oura directly.) - **Nighttime Blood Pressure isn't shown** — it's not yet exposed in the Oura API. - **Implausible heart-rate spikes are filtered** — readings above a plausible physiological ceiling are dropped as PPG sensor artifacts, so peaks and averages reflect real beats. - **"Walk Equivalent" isn't literal walking distance** — it's Oura's energy burn expressed as an equivalent walking distance, not steps × stride. - **Max HR and training zones are estimates, not measured** — heart-rate zones use the Tanaka formula (`208 − 0.7 × age`) personalized with your resting HR (Karvonen), not a measured maximum. A true personal max needs a max-effort test; your observed peak usually underestimates it. - **Some Oura metrics require extra API scopes** — Cardiovascular Age and Resilience exist in the Oura API but need additional scopes on your token. If your token lacks them, those tiles stay empty. - **Data timing follows Oura's API, not the wall clock** — Oura frequently backdates and later revises data: a night's sleep isn't written until after you wake and sync, and Oura may insert or update readings for prior days hours later. So the most recent day or two can be incomplete or change as Oura fills them in — dashboards are only as current as both the last ingest run and Oura's own timing. ## License Apache-2.0 · Copyright 2026 Tony Vincent","backgroundColor":"rgba(15,17,23,0.50)"}}},"dataSources":{},"inputs":{},"layout":{"type":"absolute","options":{"width":1440,"height":900,"display":"auto","backgroundImage":{"x":0,"y":0,"src":"/static/app/oura_health/bg_health.svg","sizeType":"cover"}},"structure":[{"item":"about_md","type":"block","position":{"x":20,"y":20,"w":1400,"h":860}}],"globalInputs":[]}}
